Class HumanPlayer

java.lang.Object
com.cluegame.players.Player
com.cluegame.players.HumanPlayer
public class HumanPlayer extends Player
Represents a human-controlled player in Clue! Movement, suggestions and accusations are made via console input.
Author:
Thanh Shaw

  • Constructor Details

    • HumanPlayer

      public HumanPlayer(String name, String token, int startRow, int startCol)
      Constructs a human player with a name, token and starting position.
      Parameters:
      name - the player's name
      token - the player's token identifier
      startRow - starting row on the board
      startCol - starting column on the board

    • HumanPlayer

      public HumanPlayer(String name, String token, int startRow, int startCol, Scanner scanner)
      Constructs a human player with a custom Scanner (useful for testing).
      Parameters:
      name - the player's name
      token - the player's token identifier
      startRow - starting row on the board
      startCol - starting column on the board
      scanner - the Scanner to use for input

  • Method Details

    • takeTurn

      public void takeTurn(Board board, Dice dice, List<Player> allPlayers)
      Takes a turn by rolling the dice and moving step-by-step through corridors. The player chooses direction each step via console input. If the player is in a room they may use a secret passage or exit through a door before rolling.
      Specified by:
      takeTurn in class Player
      Parameters:
      board - the game board
      dice - the dice to roll for movement
      allPlayers - all players (for corridor occupancy checks)

    • makeSuggestion

      public Suggestion makeSuggestion()
      Lets the human player make a suggestion via console input. The room is automatically set to the room the player is currently in. The player picks a suspect and weapon from numbered menus.
      Specified by:
      makeSuggestion in class Player
      Returns:
      the Suggestion, or null if the player declines

    • makeAccusation

      public Accusation makeAccusation()
      Asks the human player if they want to make an accusation. If yes, they pick a suspect, weapon and room (can be any room, not just current).
      Specified by:
      makeAccusation in class Player
      Returns:
      the Accusation, or null if the player declines

    • chooseSuggestionCard

      public Card chooseSuggestionCard(List<Card> matchingCards, String askerName)
      When this player must disprove a suggestion, lets them choose which matching card to show. If only one card matches it is shown automatically.
      Specified by:
      chooseSuggestionCard in class Player
      Parameters:
      matchingCards - cards in hand that match the suggestion
      askerName - the player who made the suggestion
      Returns:
      the card chosen to reveal

    • seeDisprovalCard

      public void seeDisprovalCard(Card card, String fromPlayerName)
      Called when another player shows this player a card to disprove their suggestion. Displays the card to the console.
      Specified by:
      seeDisprovalCard in class Player
      Parameters:
      card - the card being shown
      fromPlayerName - the name of the player showing the card

    • readInt

      private int readInt(int min, int max)
      Reads an integer from the console within the given range (inclusive). Keeps prompting until the player enters a valid number.
      Parameters:
      min - minimum acceptable value
      max - maximum acceptable value
      Returns:
      the valid integer entered by the player

    • isDoorBlocked

      private boolean isDoorBlocked(Square door, List<Player> allPlayers)
      Checks if a door square is occupied by another player in the corridor.
      Parameters:
      door - the door square to check
      allPlayers - all players in the game
      Returns:
      true if another player is standing on that door square

    • getHandSummary

      private String getHandSummary()
      Returns a short comma-separated summary of the player's hand.
      Returns:
      hand contents as a single line string
